summary: PR #71 deferred finding 1 (forge-cli issue close --reason completed rejected) is resolved. forge-cli issue close --dry-run --format json (forge-cli 0.17.6) now returns ok=true without --reason; backend plan is gh issue close <id>.
evidence: Dry-run verification: $ forge-cli issue close 99999 --repo graysurf/agent-runtime-kit --dry-run --format json → ok=true, plan=['gh','issue','close','99999']. With --reason completed: ok=false, code=unknown-subcommand. All 6 source SKILL.md.tera files and 12 golden expected/SKILL.md snapshots now show 0 occurrences of '--reason completed'.

summary: PR #71 deferred finding 2 (forge-cli issue view --format json omits comments) is resolved on the GitHub path. The Step 10 closeout block now uses gh issue view --json body,comments which returns both fields directly.
evidence: Dry-run verification: $ forge-cli issue view 67 --format json --dry-run shows backend plan ['gh','issue','view','67','--json','number,url,state,title,labels,assignees,body'] — no comments field. $ gh issue view 67 --json body,comments returns {body: string, comments: [..]}. The new Step 10 block reads body and comments from the gh payload directly.

summary: GitLab path uses glab issue view --comments --output json and reshapes the payload via jq '{body: .description, comments: (.notes // .comments // [])}'. The jq fallback chain handles both common glab naming conventions (.notes vs .comments) but is not verified against a live GitLab issue in this repo (which lives on GitHub).
evidence: glab issue view --help confirms --comments and --output json flags exist. The actual JSON field name for body in glab's output is .description; the comments array name varies by glab version (older glab used .notes, newer may use .comments). The reshape jq normalizes both into the {body, comments} shape that plan-issue record --comments-json expects.
recommendation: Mark as residual risk for the GitLab path until a live GitLab project exercises this code path. Out of scope to verify in this PR because the canonical exercise (chained closeout against the follow-up's own tracking issue) lives on GitHub. Track via a heuristic-inbox case for future GitLab-side validation.

summary: All six modified SKILL bodies now document WHY they use gh/glab as a comments-fetch substitute (forge-cli 0.17.6 omits comments) in both Prereqs and inline comments above the closeout block. This makes the workaround discoverable for future readers who might otherwise try to switch back to forge-cli issue view.
evidence: Each of the 6 bodies carries a Prereqs note explaining the gh/glab requirement, plus an inline comment before the closeout fetch command explaining the same reason in operational context. Future docs-fix PRs that swap to a comments-aware forge-cli (once nils-cli grows it) can grep on 'forge-cli issue view --format json' to find every site to update.

summary: The end-to-end re-validation is Sprint 2.4: open the PR, merge, and exercise the (corrected) Step 10 chained closeout against this plan's own tracking issue #73. Static gates (plan-tooling validate, grep gate, scripts/ci/all.sh) all pass on the current branch.
evidence: plan-tooling validate exit 0. grep -rc -- '--reason completed' core/skills/ tests/golden/ sums to 0. bash scripts/ci/all.sh exit 0 (positions 1-10).
recommendation: Sprint 2.4 is the canonical e2e validation; record success against issue #73 in the execution-state Validation table.

summary: The Workflow section (steps 1–8) does not repeat the gh prereq inline. The Entrypoint block above documents it, but a reader who jumps straight to the Workflow steps might miss it.
evidence: Entrypoint block (line 47–84) carries the gh fetch + jq derivation. Workflow steps 1–8 (line 103–118) reference 'plan-issue record audit' / 'closeout-gate' without restating the body/comments derivation. Same pattern in dispatch-plan-closeout.
recommendation: Defer. The Entrypoint block is the canonical command reference; the Workflow is a higher-level outline that intentionally elides exact commands. Adding the prereq there would duplicate maintenance surface.
